sections.paragraph:
to thrust; Lat. trudo (which verb has the same radicals); hence to follow on continually one after another, Pro 19:13 27:15 , דֶּלֶף טֹרֵד “a thrusting dropping,” i.e. dropping continually, drop coming close upon drop. (Arab. طرد to thrust, to push forward, IV. one thing to follow another, see Schult. ad Prov. loc. cit.; Taur. ad Ham. page 516. Syr. and Chald. טְרַד i.q. Conj. I.)

Hence pr.n. מַטְרֵד.
